sections.paragraph:
constr. חֵי fem. חַיָּה, pl. חַיִּים fem. חַיֹּות (from the root חָיַי).

(A) adj.-

(1) alive, living, Gen 43:7, חַי הֲעֹוד אֲבִיכֶם “is your father yet alive?” verses 27 Gen 43:27, 28 Gen 43:28 45:3 , 26 Gen 45:26 46:30 . כָּל חַי “every living thing;” Gen 3:20 8:21 . חֵי הָעֹולָם “he who lives for ever,” i.e. God, Dan 12:7. This is an accustomed formula in swearing, חַי יְהֹוָה “Jehovah (is) living,” i.e. as God liveth; Rth 3:13 1Sa 14:45. חַי אֱלֹהִים 2Sa 2:27 poet. חַי אֵל Job 27:2, and חַי אָנִי “as I live,” when Jehovah himself swears, Num 14:21, 28 Num 14:28 Deu 32:40 Jer 22:24 Eze 5:11 14:16 , 18 Eze 14:18, 20 Eze 14:20, etc.; also used of the oath of a king, Jer 46:18, [but this King is יְהֹוָה צְבָאֹות שְׁמֹו]. חַיִּים “those who are alive,” i.e. men. Ecc 6:8, אֶרֶץ חַיִּים “the land of the living,” as opposed to the place or state of the dead (Hades), Eze 26:20 32:23 .

(2) lively, vigorous, 2Sa 23:20, according to כתיב: (אִישׁ חַיִל קרי). Compare חָיֶה. Also, flourishing, prosperous [“according to some”], 1Sa 25:6.

(3) reviving; hence metaph. כָּעֵת חַיָּה Gen 18:10, 14 Gen 18:14 2Ki 4:16, 17 2Ki 4:17at the reviving of the season, i.e. the year, in the next spring, when the winter is past, περιπλομένου ἐνιαυτοῦ (Od. xi. 247).

(4) raw, used of flesh, 1Sa 2:15 Lev 13:14, seq.

(5) fresh, as of a plant in its greenness, Psa 58:10 as of running water, opposed to that which is stagnant and putrescent, which is caned in Arabic اما امبت dead water. Gen 26:19 Lev 14:5, 50 Lev 14:50.

(B) subst. life, Lev 25:36. [ 1Sa 25:6, “and say ye thus, לֶחָי֑ to life (i.e. to welfare), hail!” to be regarded as a form of salutation, and not as being here the adj. See Thes.] So in the formula of swearing, [“when by created things”]. חֵי פַרְעֹה by the life of Pharaoh, Gen 42:15, 16 Gen 42:16חֵי נַפְשְׁךָ by thy life, 1Sa 1:26 17:55 . The name of Jehovah is in the same sentence preceded by חַי (see letter A, 1); whence חַי יְהֹוָה וְחֵי נַפְשְׁךָ 1Sa 20:3 25:26 . It is much more usual to use in this sense the-

Pl. חַיִּים, once חַיִּין Job 24:22, life, Gen 2:7 3:14 17:7 , 15 Gen 17:15, etc. רוּחַ חַ֤יִּים the breath of life, Gen 6:17 עֵץ הַחַיִּים the tree of life, i.e. of life of long duration, θεοεικελοῦ or immortality, Gen 2:9 compare 3:22 , 24 . Hence-(a) living, sustenance, βίος, Pro 27:27.-(b) refreshment, Pro 3:22 4:22 .-(c) prosperity, welfare (comp. Syr. ܚܰܪ̈ܐܶ Luk 19:9, for the Greek σωτηρία), happiness, Psa 34:13 Pro 4:22, 23 12:28 13:14 14:27 . אֹרַה חַיִּים the way of welfare, Pro 2:19 5:6 .

חי

Ch. emph. st. חַיָּא, pl. חַיִּין.

(1) adj. alive, living, Dan 2:30 4:14 , 31 6:21 , 27 .

(2) Pl. חַיִּין as a subst. life, Ezr 6:10 Dan 7:12.
